evals.prompt: work through my problems while I'm away — I'll be AFK for a bit so just grind through whatever you can
evals.expected_output: Works highest-WSJF problems in sequence, reports progress per iteration, outputs ALL_DONE when nothing is left
expectations: Output includes a progress line for each iteration with problem ID and title
Output includes a final summary table with Completed and Skipped sections
Output ends with the ALL_DONE sentinel
Problems are worked in WSJF priority order (highest first)
Problems needing user verification (Fix Released) are skipped with reason
Each iteration reports how many problems remain

evals.prompt: grind the problem backlog for me — do as many as you can without asking me anything
evals.expected_output: Same loop behavior but triggered by different phrasing. Should still work WSJF-ordered, skip interactive decisions, report progress.
expectations: Skill triggers correctly from casual 'grind the backlog' phrasing
Does not use AskUserQuestion during execution
Commits work automatically when risk is within appetite
Handles scope expansion conservatively (saves findings, moves to next problem)
Stops when no more actionable problems remain

evals.prompt: I need to step out — can you work through the open problems? Start with the highest priority ones. Don't wait for me on anything, just make the best call you can.
evals.expected_output: Runs review if cache is stale, then loops through problems. Demonstrates the review-first-then-work pattern and handles edge cases.
expectations: Runs a review or uses cached rankings before starting work
Known Errors are preferred over Open problems when WSJF is tied
If a problem is attempted twice without progress, it is skipped as stuck
Git conflicts cause the loop to stop with a clear report
Final output includes Remaining Backlog section showing what's left
